detection.notes: No strong deterministic line structure detected

routing.notes: Text-like content with weak or no deterministic pattern

family_info.notes: Direct free-form text lane from free_text_log

records.evidence_text: Equipment: EQP_IMP_01 (Ion Implanter); Date: 13 April 2026; Shift: Morning (0600-1400); Engineer on duty: Tan Wei Liang
The operator ran a standard chamber conditioning cycle before loading the first lot | the ion implanter was brought online following overnight preventive maintenance

supporting_observations.evidence_text: the ion implanter was brought online following overnight preventive maintenance

records.evidence_text: At approximately 07:45, the ion implanter was brought online
Lot LOT_0045 was loaded at 08:10. The lot contains 25 wafers scheduled for phosphorus implantation at 40 keV with a dose of 1e13 cm-2.

extra.timing_context: load time given; implantation described as scheduled

records.evidence_text: Recipe used is IMP_RCP_PHOS_40K.
08:12 - Wafer 1 (WFR_0001) loaded into beam path. Pre-implant alignment completed within spec.
Beam current measured at 4.2 mA, within the acceptable range of 3.8 to 4.5 mA.
08:14 - Implant started on WFR_0001.
Dose rate stable at approximately 1.8e12 ions/cm2/s.
08:17 - Implant on WFR_0001 completed.
Actual dose confirmed at 1.02e13 cm-2.
Wafer unloaded.
08:18 - Wafer 2 (WFR_0002) loaded. Pre-implant alignment OK.
Operator adjusted suppression voltage and beam current recovered to 4.0 mA by 08:22.

extra.context: Beam current initially at 4.1 mA but dropped to 3.2 mA around 08:21.

records.evidence_text: 08:20 - Implant started.
08:25 - Implant on WFR_0002 completed.
Final dose: 9.85e12 cm-2. Slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band.
Wafer accepted.

extra.acceptance_basis: dose slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band

records.evidence_text: 08:26 - Wafer 3 (WFR_0003) loaded.
Operator re-ran alignment. Second attempt passed.

extra.context: after initial Y-axis offset exceeded threshold

records.evidence_text: Pre-implant alignment showed an offset of 0.4 mm in Y-axis, which is above the 0.3 mm threshold.
08:29 - Implant started on WFR_0003.
08:32 - FAULT: High vacuum pressure spike detected in the end-station.
Beam was automatically cut off. Implant halted at approximately 40% completion.
Pressure reading was 3.8e-5 Pa, exceeding the process limit of 2.0e-5 Pa.
Found the gate valve partially obstructed.
Valve was manually cycled. | 08:33 - Operator initiated vacuum recovery procedure. | Chamber was isolated and turbo pump speed checked.

supporting_observations.evidence_text: Chamber was isolated and turbo pump speed checked.

records.evidence_text: 08:40 - Vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.
System resumed standby state.

extra.context: Occurred after vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.

records.evidence_text: Lot LOT_0045 processing paused pending engineering disposition.
Engineering decision: WFR_0003 quarantined for dose non-conformance. Wafer will be sent for metrology review.
09:02 - Wafer 4 (WFR_0004) loaded. All pre-checks passed.

extra.sequence_note: Pre-checks reported as passed at time of load

records.evidence_text: 09:04 - Implant started.
Beam current stable at 4.2 mA throughout run.
09:08 - Implant on WFR_0004 completed.
Dose confirmed at 1.01e13 cm-2.
Wafer accepted.
Processing continued normally through WFR_0025. Final summary: Total wafers processed: 25 ... Total process time: approx. 4 hours 20 minutes
Wafers accepted: 23; Wafers quarantined: 1 (WFR_0003, partial dose); Wafers rejected: 1 (WFR_0002 marginal, held for review)
Shift handover completed at 13:50 to PM shift engineer Ravi Kumar.

detailed_records.evidence_text: Equipment: EQP_IMP_01 (Ion Implanter); Date: 13 April 2026; Shift: Morning (0600-1400); Engineer on duty: Tan Wei Liang
The operator ran a standard chamber conditioning cycle before loading the first lot
the ion implanter was brought online following overnight preventive maintenance
At approximately 07:45, the ion implanter was brought online
Lot LOT_0045 was loaded at 08:10. The lot contains 25 wafers scheduled for phosphorus implantation at 40 keV with a dose of 1e13 cm-2.

extra.timing_context: load time given; implantation described as scheduled

detailed_records.evidence_text: Recipe used is IMP_RCP_PHOS_40K.
08:12 - Wafer 1 (WFR_0001) loaded into beam path. Pre-implant alignment completed within spec.
Beam current measured at 4.2 mA, within the acceptable range of 3.8 to 4.5 mA.
08:14 - Implant started on WFR_0001.
Dose rate stable at approximately 1.8e12 ions/cm2/s.
08:17 - Implant on WFR_0001 completed.
Actual dose confirmed at 1.02e13 cm-2.
Wafer unloaded.
08:18 - Wafer 2 (WFR_0002) loaded. Pre-implant alignment OK.
Operator adjusted suppression voltage and beam current recovered to 4.0 mA by 08:22.

extra.context: Beam current initially at 4.1 mA but dropped to 3.2 mA around 08:21.

detailed_records.evidence_text: 08:20 - Implant started.
08:25 - Implant on WFR_0002 completed.
Final dose: 9.85e12 cm-2. Slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band.
Wafer accepted.

extra.acceptance_basis: dose slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band

detailed_records.evidence_text: 08:26 - Wafer 3 (WFR_0003) loaded.
Operator re-ran alignment. Second attempt passed.

extra.context: after initial Y-axis offset exceeded threshold

detailed_records.evidence_text: Pre-implant alignment showed an offset of 0.4 mm in Y-axis, which is above the 0.3 mm threshold.
08:29 - Implant started on WFR_0003.
08:32 - FAULT: High vacuum pressure spike detected in the end-station.
Beam was automatically cut off. Implant halted at approximately 40% completion.
Pressure reading was 3.8e-5 Pa, exceeding the process limit of 2.0e-5 Pa.
Estimated partial dose on WFR_0003 is approximately 4.0e12 cm-2.
Found the gate valve partially obstructed.
Valve was manually cycled.
08:33 - Operator initiated vacuum recovery procedure.
Chamber was isolated and turbo pump speed checked.
08:40 - Vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.
System resumed standby state.

extra.context: Occurred after vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.

detailed_records.evidence_text: Lot LOT_0045 processing paused pending engineering disposition.
Engineering decision: WFR_0003 quarantined for dose non-conformance. Wafer will be sent for metrology review.
09:02 - Wafer 4 (WFR_0004) loaded. All pre-checks passed.

extra.sequence_note: Pre-checks reported as passed at time of load

detailed_records.evidence_text: 09:04 - Implant started.
Beam current stable at 4.2 mA throughout run.
09:08 - Implant on WFR_0004 completed.
Dose confirmed at 1.01e13 cm-2.
Wafer accepted.
Processing continued normally through WFR_0025. Final summary: Total wafers processed: 25 ... Total process time: approx. 4 hours 20 minutes
Wafers accepted: 23; Wafers quarantined: 1 (WFR_0003, partial dose); Wafers rejected: 1 (WFR_0002 marginal, held for review)
No additional alarms after 08:40
Shift handover completed at 13:50 to PM shift engineer Ravi Kumar.

records.evidence_text: Equipment: EQP_IMP_01 (Ion Implanter); Date: 13 April 2026; Shift: Morning (0600-1400); Engineer on duty: Tan Wei Liang
the ion implanter was brought online following overnight preventive maintenance
At approximately 07:45, the ion implanter was brought online
The operator ran a standard chamber conditioning cycle before loading the first lot
Lot LOT_0045 was loaded at 08:10. The lot contains 25 wafers scheduled for phosphorus implantation at 40 keV with a dose of 1e13 cm-2.

extra.timing_context: load time given; implantation described as scheduled

records.evidence_text: Recipe used is IMP_RCP_PHOS_40K.
08:12 - Wafer 1 (WFR_0001) loaded into beam path. Pre-implant alignment completed within spec.
Beam current measured at 4.2 mA, within the acceptable range of 3.8 to 4.5 mA.
08:14 - Implant started on WFR_0001.
Dose rate stable at approximately 1.8e12 ions/cm2/s.
08:17 - Implant on WFR_0001 completed.
Actual dose confirmed at 1.02e13 cm-2.
Wafer unloaded.
08:18 - Wafer 2 (WFR_0002) loaded. Pre-implant alignment OK.
08:20 - Implant started.
The system issued a beam current instability warning.
Operator adjusted suppression voltage and beam current recovered to 4.0 mA by 08:22.

extra.context: Beam current initially at 4.1 mA but dropped to 3.2 mA around 08:21.

records.evidence_text: 08:25 - Implant on WFR_0002 completed.
Final dose: 9.85e12 cm-2. Slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band.
Wafer accepted.

extra.acceptance_basis: dose slightly below target but within 5% tolerance band

records.evidence_text: 08:26 - Wafer 3 (WFR_0003) loaded.
Pre-implant alignment showed an offset of 0.4 mm in Y-axis, which is above the 0.3 mm threshold.
Operator re-ran alignment. Second attempt passed.

extra.context: after initial Y-axis offset exceeded threshold

records.evidence_text: 08:29 - Implant started on WFR_0003.
08:32 - FAULT: High vacuum pressure spike detected in the end-station.
Beam was automatically cut off. Implant halted at approximately 40% completion.
Pressure reading was 3.8e-5 Pa, exceeding the process limit of 2.0e-5 Pa.
Estimated partial dose on WFR_0003 is approximately 4.0e12 cm-2.
08:33 - Operator initiated vacuum recovery procedure.
Chamber was isolated and turbo pump speed checked.
Found the gate valve partially obstructed.
Valve was manually cycled.
08:40 - Vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.
System resumed standby state.

extra.context: Occurred after vacuum recovered to 1.4e-5 Pa.

records.evidence_text: Engineering decision: WFR_0003 quarantined for dose non-conformance. Wafer will be sent for metrology review.
Lot LOT_0045 processing paused pending engineering disposition.
09:02 - Wafer 4 (WFR_0004) loaded. All pre-checks passed.

extra.sequence_note: Pre-checks reported as passed at time of load

records.evidence_text: 09:04 - Implant started.
Beam current stable at 4.2 mA throughout run.
09:08 - Implant on WFR_0004 completed.
Dose confirmed at 1.01e13 cm-2.
Wafer accepted.
Processing continued normally through WFR_0025. Final summary: Total wafers processed: 25 ... Total process time: approx. 4 hours 20 minutes
Wafers accepted: 23; Wafers quarantined: 1 (WFR_0003, partial dose); Wafers rejected: 1 (WFR_0002 marginal, held for review)
No additional alarms after 08:40
Shift handover completed at 13:50 to PM shift engineer Ravi Kumar.
